summary: The public guarded one-command path composes the existing production runtime connectors and independent review lifecycle into a bounded, resumable run-session DAG. Exact-HEAD evidence covers managed-worktree implementation, typed stop/resume, needs_changes repair and fresh independent re-review, current-HEAD final preparation, and the explicit human boundary for PR creation, merge, waiver, and material external side effects.
inspection_summary: Inspected the public CLI composition, Codex production bridge, guarded run-session state machine, one-command action owners, production connector policies, independent review lifecycle, action replay tests, production-shaped E2E, and exact-HEAD verification/conformance artifacts. The implementation remains inside the run-session boundary with no new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependency. Runtime implementation is constrained to the managed worktree with workspace-write, local-only capabilities and approval_policy=never; review is separately identified, read-only, current-surface-bound, lifecycle-closed, and is rerun after a repair advances HEAD. Only final_prepare can transition to pr_ready, and it requires the prepared HEAD to equal the persisted current HEAD.
judgment_delta: A stale reused review and older pr-prepare artifact initially left current-surface coverage uncertain; exact-HEAD verification evidence refreshed at 2026-07-24T04:02:34.662Z, direct source inspection, and the 4/4 production-shaped E2E close that uncertainty for this role.

The integration could have duplicated the already-merged connector and review systems; import and composition inspection instead shows createProductionRuntimeConnectors and the existing independent-review orchestrator are reused through the guarded run-session boundary.

The repair loop could have accepted a stale review or stale preparation; journal matching, repaired-HEAD review invalidation, runtime review provenance checks, HEAD rebinding, and final_prepare equality checks demonstrate fail-closed current-surface behavior.

The command could have crossed the external-authority boundary; source prompts, CLI contract tests, and E2E observations keep PR creation, merge, waiver, deploy, publish, and other material external side effects outside autonomous execution.

Regression and path-surface concerns are resolved by 290/290 targeted tests, 4/4 integration tests, 4/4 E2E tests, clean diff checking, conformance improvement from 81 to 80 total baseline violations, and zero new run-session-to-CLI reverse dependencies.
